design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through linear missions defeating waves of enemies and bosses using directional attack system (left/right attack buttons) and environmental weapons; avenge Marian's death through increasing difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health depletion from enemy encounters balanced against timer pressure per mission; cooperative vs competitive dynamic in two-player mode
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as martial arts brothers Lee avenging their girlfriend's murder, mastering combat moves and adapting to varied enemy types and boss patterns
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Directional attack system (replacing simple punch/kick with left/right contextual attacks) creates skill expression; NES version gates final mission by difficulty tier, enforcing progression hierarchy; cooperative play reintroduced after missing from first NES port

difficulty.lives_system: Single life meter that depletes on hits; one life lost when meter reaches zero or player falls off screen/into hazards
difficulty.continues_system: Japanese version: continues available by default. International versions: requires cheat code entry at GAME OVER screen that changes every third stage; player returns to main menu to resume at last stage with ability to change difficulty
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing; progress resets to stage start on death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty tiers (PRACTICE/EASY, WARRIOR/NORMAL, SUPREME MASTER/DIFFICULT) with regional gating: international versions restrict PRACTICE to Mission 3, WARRIOR to Mission 8, SUPREME MASTER unlocks all 9 missions. Japanese version allows all 9 missions on any difficulty. Disappearing platform patterns in Mission 6 scale with difficulty (EASY: all platforms visible; NORMAL: two at a time; DIFFICULT: one at a time). Enemy health and aggression increase on higher difficulties

progression.unlock_mechanism: Difficulty-gated mission progression in international versions (lower difficulties cap at earlier missions); boss defeats unlock next mission
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty tiers with distinct enemy behavior and platforming challenges
Cooperative two-player mode vs single-player changes encounter design
Varied weapon pickups (shovels, knives, whips, grenades, environmental objects) create tactical options
Regional differences between Japanese and international versions encourage replays

world_building_techniques: Pre-mission cutscenes with character close-ups and environmental shots establishing each location
Regional narrative differences: opening cutscene features Billy pose/yell in international versions, omitted in Japanese version
Enemy character designs (Burnov's vanishing body and materialization, Willy's non-droppable machine gun) embed story through mechanics
Location progression (heliport → lumber storehouse → farm → hideout) creates implicit narrative geography of gang territory

boss_design.philosophy: Escalating mechanical complexity with signature weapons/abilities: early bosses use standard attacks, later bosses feature unique move sets (Chin Taimei's kali sticks, Willy's machine gun, Doppelganger's energy drain). Some bosses gain new abilities when fought again (Burnov rematerializes mid-fight)
notable_bosses: Burnov (Mission 1): large tanky enemy whose body vanishes on defeat but can rematerialize later; no weapon drop
Abore (Mission 2): large blond fighter in distinctive white/red outfit
Chin Taimei (Mission 3): agile martial artist wielding kali sticks that cannot be knocked from hands; rewards pattern recognition
Willy (Mission 4): Black Warriors leader with machine gun (never drops); final retaliation boss
Doppelganger (Final Battle): shadowy clone of player character performing all player moves plus energy drain ability; one per active player

faction_indicators: Black Warriors: organized gang with named ranks (Williams/Roper: low-ranking members; Linda: only female member; Oharra/Bolo: mid-tier fighters; Abore/Chin Taimei/Willy: leadership hierarchy)
Lee Brothers: external avenging force, not part of gang structure
danger_signals: Willy's retaliatory murder of Marian establishes gang as lethally organized threat
Escalating enemy armor/weapons per mission (grenades in later stages; machine gun wielder as final boss)
Hideout location as final mission indicates gang consolidation of territory
Doppelganger battle symbolizes internalized threat after defeating external gang leadership
environmental_storytelling: Heliport mission entry establishes aerial insertion into gang territory
Lumber storehouse and farm stages suggest gang resource control/extraction from industrial areas
Hideout finale represents penetration into gang's central command structure
Weapon variety (industrial tools: shovels; medieval: whips; modern: grenades/machine gun) implies gang mercenary composition from different backgrounds

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Double Dragon II NES article)
